perSite.workScenario: A legacy/poured (v1) molecule. Callers: order_dispatch.go:1410, cmd_order.go:705 (non-v2 order whose root compiles to type=molecule), cmd_formula.go:711 (gc formula cook of a legacy formula). The root is coerced to Type=molecule with only formula_hash/source/idempotency/vars and NO graph marker, so classifyFields falls through to default => ClassWork. graphWorkflow=false, so molecule.go:617 stamps no gc.root_bead_id on children => every child is ClassWork. The whole molecule is homogeneous ClassWork.
perSite.graphScenario: A graph.v2 workflow (or root-only wisp). Callers: sling.go:1287 (primary), drain.go:940 item formulas (hard-rejected at :927 unless graph.v2), order/wisp paths. compile.go:343-345 stamps the root gc.kind=workflow + contract=graph.v2 => root ClassGraph; graphWorkflow=true so molecule.go:617 stamps gc.root_bead_id on EVERY child => every child ClassGraph. A wisp root (gc.kind=wisp) is ClassGraph and rootOnly (no descendants). The whole molecule is homogeneous ClassGraph.
perSite.verdict: HOMOGENEOUS-PER-CALL. One Instantiate call is NEVER genuinely mixed in any path a caller or transform produces. The class of the entire molecule is decided by ONE bit at compile time: whether the formula declares the v2 graph compiler (UsesGraphCompiler -> graphWorkflow), and that bit drives the root AND all children coherently. The compile-time lock (compile.go:173 validateExplicitGraphCompilerRequirement, run at Stage 9.5 before retry/ralph expansion) REJECTS any step that uses a graph construct or carries a forcing gc.kind unless the formula declares v2 — and declaring v2 flips graphWorkflow=true. So a legacy molecule is provably forbidden from containing a system-meaningful graph step. The only mixed case is a non-functional hand-authored pathology (literally writing gc.kind=fanout/tally/spec — the 3 values absent from the forcing set — on a step of a legacy formula); no caller or transform generates it. Confirmed at molecule.go:617. VERDICT: caller can pick the store; no per-id routing object required. All 5 callers already hold the recipe and the class bit is graphroute.IsCompiledGraphWorkflow(recipe) (sling.go:1247 already computes it).

perSite.site: molecule.CloseSubtree / ListSubtree -> store.CloseAll (internal/molecule/cleanup.go:144, ListSubtree:24-75). Callers: sling_attachment.go:154, wisp_autoclose.go:180, molecule_autoclose.go:203
perSite.workScenario: CONCRETE and TYPICAL: a v1 poured/legacy molecule attached to a source bead via molecule_id (sling_core.go arm taken only when !GraphWorkflow). Root is type=molecule with no graph metadata (ClassWork); its step descendants cannot carry gc.kind (forbidden by the compile lock) so are never stamped gc.root_bead_id (ClassWork). The id set {molecule root + step children} is all-ClassWork.
perSite.graphScenario: NEVER reaches THIS site. graph.v2 workflows are diverted BEFORE molecule.CloseSubtree: CloseAttachedSubtree sends IsWorkflowAttachment roots to sourceworkflow.CloseWorkflowSubtree (sling_attachment.go:151-152) — a DIFFERENT call. A standalone wisp (gc.kind=wisp, ClassGraph) is rootOnly with no descendants and is never a molecule_id attachment; an attached root-only wisp is de-graphed first (privatizeAttachedRootOnlyWisp sets type=molecule -> ClassWork) before attachment.
perSite.verdict: HOMOGENEOUS-PER-CALL, and the class is CONSTANT (always ClassWork). The prior recon's 'subtree spanning both stores' claim conflates molecule.CloseSubtree with the graph-workflow closer; they are different functions. Confirmed at sling_attachment.go:151-154: the workflow branch diverts to sourceworkflow.CloseWorkflowSubtree; only the type=molecule (legacy, ClassWork) branch reaches molecule.CloseSubtree. The reachable root is always ClassWork, so the caller passes the work store unconditionally — no per-id routing object and no class computation needed. churnFiles: none.

perSite.site: drain + convoy membership: drain.go (convoy.Members at :194, trackDrainMember/TrackItem at :889, drain-unit convoy Create at :855) AND the shared convoy primitive convoy/membership.go (Members:68, TrackItem:24) — also hit by graphv2.CreateSingleItemInputConvoy (invocation.go:382-407)
perSite.workScenario: The drained/tracked MEMBERS are plain work beads (type=task/bug etc.), classified by their own type => ClassWork. The user's slung source bead is the canonical member.
perSite.graphScenario: The convoy that tracks them is synthetic: drain-unit convoy carries gc.synthetic=true + synthetic_kind=drain-unit-convoy (drain.go:845-846), and the graph.v2 input convoy carries gc.synthetic=true (invocation.go:393). isSyntheticConvoy => ClassGraph (classify.go:126,156). The drain control bead, item-root, and unit convoy are all ClassGraph.
perSite.verdict: GENUINELY MIXED-CLASS IN ONE INVOCATION. This is the real cross-store site and the prior recon was RIGHT here. convoy.TrackItem(store, convoyID, memberID) does store.Get(memberID) [ClassWork] AND store.DepAdd(convoyID [ClassGraph], memberID [ClassWork], 'tracks') in a single call with a single store param. convoy.Members(store, convoyID) does store.DepList on the ClassGraph convoy then store.Get on each ClassWork member. A single store cannot satisfy both halves unless it is the router. The code ALREADY proves this is a live, hit scenario: drain.go:259 drainUnresolvedMemberError reads 'parent convoy %s has unresolved or cross-store member %s' — when the single store can't Get a cross-store member it degrades to an unresolved placeholder and the drain hard-fails. Confirmed end-to-end: invocation.go:404 (synthetic convoy tracks the work target), drain.go:889 (synthetic unit convoy tracks the work member), membership.go:68-115 (single-store Members). The convoy<->member tracks edge inherently straddles work/graph. callerCanPick: NO. churnFiles: internal/convoy/membership.go, internal/dispatch/drain.go, internal/graphv2/invocation.go.

sitesNeedingRoutingObject: drain + convoy-membership boundary (convoy.Members / convoy.TrackItem in internal/convoy/membership.go, exercised by both drain-unit convoys in internal/dispatch/drain.go and graph.v2 input convoys in internal/graphv2/invocation.go)
revisedDecision: It collapses to ONE genuine routing site, and that site is the convoy-membership boundary — not 'drain' as a whole and not the two molecule sites. molecule.Instantiate and molecule.CloseSubtree are both homogeneous-per-call: the class of an entire molecule/subtree is fixed by a single compile-time bit (UsesGraphCompiler/graphWorkflow), enforced by the validateExplicitGraphCompilerRequirement lock, so no single invocation ever spans classes and the caller can pass the correct store. The ONLY irreducible cross-class invocation is the tracks edge between a synthetic (ClassGraph) convoy and its (ClassWork) members, expressed through convoy.TrackItem and convoy.Members. This means: (1) The routing surface is much smaller than the prior recon implied — it is the convoy primitive's membership functions, two functions in internal/convoy/membership.go, not three call sites scattered across molecule/dispatch. (2) Options for the owner: (a) Keep coordrouter ONLY for the convoy-membership boundary — let convoy.Members/TrackItem/HasTrack/UntrackItem/TrackingConvoysForItem receive the router so the convoy<->member Get/DepAdd can resolve across both backends, and give every other site a single class-picked store. This is the minimal, lowest-blast-radius shape. (b) Make convoy membership take a SECOND store explicitly: TrackItem(convoyStore, memberStore, convoyID, memberID) and Members(convoyStore, memberStore, convoyID) — turns the implicit two-store dependency into a typed contract, no router needed anywhere, at the cost of threading two stores through ~5 drain/invocation call sites. (c) Co-locate: stop marking input/unit convoys synthetic (fold them to ClassWork so convoy and members share the work store) — but the architecture deliberately folds synthetic convoys into ClassGraph ('travel with the graph they glue', classify.go:61-63), so this reverses a settled design decision and is the riskiest. Recommendation to evaluate: (b) is the cleanest type-safe long-term contract; (a) is the smallest diff if you want to ship the irreversible migration with minimum churn and retire coordrouter everywhere EXCEPT this one boundary.
reconCorrections: RIGHT: drain/convoy is genuinely mixed-class and irreducible — confirmed independently. A synthetic ClassGraph convoy tracking ClassWork members in a single TrackItem/Members call cannot be served by one store; the code's own drainUnresolvedMemberError ('cross-store member', drain.go:259) is direct evidence this is a real, guarded scenario.

OVERSTATED on molecule.Instantiate: the 'irreducible mixed molecule' claim is wrong for every recipe the system produces. A compile-time lock (compile.go:173, run pre-expansion at Stage 9.5) forces the whole molecule to one class via the graphWorkflow bit; the only mix is a non-functional hand-authored pathology no caller generates. Homogeneous-per-call; caller picks the store.

OVERSTATED on molecule.CloseSubtree: the 'subtree spanning both stores' claim conflated molecule.CloseSubtree with sourceworkflow.CloseWorkflowSubtree. graph.v2 roots are diverted to the latter at sling_attachment.go:151; molecule.CloseSubtree only ever sees legacy type=molecule subtrees, which are uniformly ClassWork. Homogeneous AND constant-class.

SCOPE CORRECTION: the irreducibility is not a property of 'drain' specifically — it is the shared convoy-membership primitive (internal/convoy/membership.go). The same cross-class tracks edge is created by graph.v2 input-convoy normalization (invocation.go:404) outside any drain. Routing the boundary, not drain, is the correct framing.
bottomLine: Of the 3 contested sites, only ONE genuinely needs cross-store routing, and it is narrower than 'drain': it is the convoy-membership boundary (convoy.Members / convoy.TrackItem), where a synthetic ClassGraph convoy holds a tracks edge to ClassWork members. The two molecule sites (Instantiate, CloseSubtree) are homogeneous-per-call — a compile-time lock fixes each molecule/subtree to a single class, so the caller can simply pass the right store and no routing object is required there. Decision for the owner: you do NOT need coordrouter spread across the codebase. You need it (or an explicit two-store signature) at exactly one seam — convoy membership. Pick between keeping coordrouter scoped to that one boundary (smallest diff) or giving convoy.Members/TrackItem a second explicit store (cleanest typed contract). Either way the routing surface for this irreversible live migration shrinks from 'three single-store call sites' to one well-defined primitive, which materially de-risks the ship.
